ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON AUG-06-2015 IN DEPT. 503, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF - HEARING HELD. THE COURT MADE THE FOLLOWING RULING: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR PREFERENCE IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 36(A) AND (D). THE TRIAL DATE IS OCTOBER 13, 2015 AT 11:15AM IN DEPARTMENT 503. THE 120TH DAY IS DEC. 4, 2015. THE LAST DAY FOR HEARING MSJ/MSA IS OCTOBER 8, 2015. PLAINTIFF STIPULATES THAT SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION MOTIONS CAN BE BROUGHT ON 20-DAY NOTICE, WITH 10 DAYS BEFORE THE HEARING TO FILE OPPOSITION. TIME TO RESPOND TO WRITTEN DISCOVERY THAT HAS NOT YET BEEN SERVED IS SHORTENED TO 15 DAYS. SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE IN ROOM 603 AT 9:00A.M. ON OCT. 14, 2015. FACT DISCOVERY CUT-OFF DATE IS SEPT. 25, 2015. EXPERT DISCOVERY CUT-OFF DATE IS OCT. 9, 2015. FURTHER CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE ON AUG. 26, 2015 AT 1:30PM. THERE WILL BE A MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE WITH THE COURT PURSUANT TO SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULES OF COURT RULE 5. THE DATE AND TIME OF THIS CONFERENCE WILL BE PROVIDED TO THE PARTIES BY THE COURT AT A LATER DATE. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF IS TO PREPARE AND FORWARD THE PROPOSED ORDER TO BERRY & BERRY FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM. (SEE WORD MINUTES THIS DATE FOR COMPLETE RULING.) JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON; REPORTER: ANGELA SINCLAIR OF AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 13902

RULING:
DEFENDANT J.T. THORPE & SON, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF PLAINTIFF'S STRICT LIABILITY CAUSE OF ACTION

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant J.T. Thorpe & Son, Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's strict liability cause of action is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting his strict liability cause of action. Defendant did not demonstrate that plaintiff failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive written discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support such claim. This failure precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden by negating an essential element of plaintiff's strict liability cause of action. Plaintiff's objections to Mr. Dawson's deposition testimony are sustained. However, even if Mr. Dawson's deposition testimony were admissible, it would be insufficient to negate an essential element of plaintiff's claim. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Nicole Hatler of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13730 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
